mechanism_narrative: A sustained low unemployment rate signals a robust labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, which in turn drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly positive (e.g., mean return < 0.1%) during periods when UNRATE is below its 10-year average.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the federal funds rate above a low threshold indicates tightening monetary policy, which typically dampens economic activity and equity valuations by increasing discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.5% and stays above for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:historical inverse correlation between rates and equity valuations
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns remain positive or flat (mean return > 0%) in more than 70% of triggered instances after FEDFUNDS crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust industrial production indicates broad-based economic expansion, suggesting healthy corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% MoM AND INDPRO 3-month average growth > 0.3% MoM
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative or flat (mean return < 0.05%) when both RSAFS and INDPRO show sustained strong growth as per the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment, any significant decline in the unemployment rate signals a labor market recovery, boosting investor confidence and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months AND UNRATE is above 6.0%
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative or flat (mean return < 0%) despite a significant drop in UNRATE during periods of high unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in real GDP reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which generally translates into higher corporate profits and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth correlates with corporate earnings
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are consistently negative (mean return < -0.1%) during periods of sustained GDPC1 growth (e.g., > 2% annualized).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and potential economic contraction, leading to concerns about corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 1.0% MoM
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive or flat (mean return > 0%) following a sharp decline in INDPRO in more than 65% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment, strong housing starts indicate robust consumer demand and investment, which, when combined with accommodative monetary policy, supports equity market growth over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1500 (thousands of units) AND CPIAUCSL annual growth < 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:low inflation allows for accommodative policy
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative or flat (mean return < 0%) when housing starts are high and inflation is low, as per the trigger conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in payroll employment, especially when accompanied by strong retail sales, indicates a robust consumer-driven expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by > 200k MoM AND RSAFS growth > 1.0% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:employment and sales drive consumer economy
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative or flat (mean return < 0%) despite simultaneous strong payroll and retail sales growth in more than 60% of triggered instances.
